---
title: "RLMHG09: Historical BAS matrices growth, waves 3-4 + DS wave 5 (Byrne et al.)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Fable 5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

RLMHG09 is the BAS-matrices sibling of `lrp-rlm-hg-001` (word reading): a
descriptive group-by-wave growth model for BAS matrices / non-verbal reasoning
in the Byrne, MacDonald & Buckley reading-language-memory cohort
(`study_id="rlm"`), for the three reading groups (Down syndrome, average
readers, reading-matched). Unlike its siblings, `basmat` was introduced at
**wave 3** — there is no wave-1/2 baseline — so the model runs on its own
later-wave window: a waves-3-4 complete-case core, extended by wave 5 where
children in that core were observed.

::: {.callout-important title="Descriptive natural-history, NOT an intervention effect"}

`readgrp` is a **cohort factor**, not a randomised or assigned condition, and this
model carries no treatment, baseline-as-precision, or adjustment-set structure.
Every quantity here is a **descriptive** group-by-wave expected score or growth
rate — natural-history evidence, not a causal effect. The intervention-effect
estimands live in the RLI (`lrp-rli-...`) suite.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Confirmed Beta-Binomial ceiling (#338)"}

The Beta-Binomial denominator ($N = 28$) is the instrument's confirmed
maximum (`n_trials_confirmed=True`): BAS Matrices (first edition) has 28 items (CLOSER
cognitive-measures guide; Parsons, 2014). Laws et al. (1995) used Raven's CPM
(36 items) as this cohort's matrices measure, so the sign-off records the
caveat that this column is BAS Matrices. Researched and signed off by
the data owner (2026-07-16). The shared prior scales are reused from
`lrp-rlm-hg-001`; the prior-predictive check below flags any miscalibration.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Audit baseline"}

The model is fit on the per-measure complete-case subset (subjects with BAS
matrices observed at waves 3 and 4). Because the window starts at wave 3, the
observed baseline here is **not** a paper Table 2 reproduction (Table 2 covers
the first three waves); it is the same complete-case observed-cell audit
computed from the prepared extract and written beside the posterior summaries.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Extended follow-up window (#338)"}

Wave 5 is an **extension wave** outside the complete-case rule: children in the
core subset contribute wherever this measure was observed. The wave-5 cell is
therefore an attrition-selected follow-up tail with its own per-cell `n`
(reported in the tables), and it exists only for the Down syndrome group.
Between-group quantities are taken over the common waves-3-4 window; wave 5
extends the Down-syndrome trajectory only.

:::

## Model

For child $i$ in reading group $g[i]$ at wave $t$, the bounded BAS-matrices
count (denominator $N = 28$) is Beta-Binomial with an age-free, group-by-wave
population mean and a stable per-child offset:

$$
\text{score}_{it} \sim \mathrm{BetaBinomial}(N,\ p_{it},\ \kappa_{g[i]}),
\qquad
\operatorname{logit}(p_{it}) = \eta_{g[i],\,t} + u_i .
$$

$\eta_{g,t}$ is the population expected score for each **supported**
group-by-wave cell (waves 3-4 in all three groups, wave 5 for the Down syndrome group only —
no prior-only cells); $u_i$ is a non-centred, **group-centred** per-child
random intercept ($u_i = (z_i - \bar z_{g[i]})\,\sigma_{\text{subject},\,g[i]}$)
so the group-by-wave level $\eta$ carries the group mean. Both the
between-child SD $\sigma_{\text{subject},\,g}$ and the measurement
overdispersion $\kappa_g$ are **indexed by group** (#338, follow-up-plan
decision 7). Deterministic quantities report the expected item score per
cell and within-group growth over the common waves-3-4 window; the
Down-syndrome extension interval and the pairwise total-growth (wave 3 to
wave 4) contrasts are summarised from the fitted cells on the children
observed at both endpoint waves.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_historical_growth.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
